zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Campbell of Loudoun於大約1512年出生在蘇格蘭艾斯爾盧度時，她的父親Hugh Campbell 15th of Loudoun - Sheriff of Ayr43歲，母親Isobel Wallace of Craigie27歲。她與Sir James "Robert" Chalmers of Gadgirth在英國蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1548年5月在英國蘇格蘭享年37歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Campbell of Loudoun于大约1512年出生在苏格兰艾斯爾盧度时，她的父亲Hugh Campbell 15th of Loudoun - Sheriff of Ayr43岁，母亲Isobel Wallace of Craigie27岁。她与Sir James "Robert" Chalmers of Gadgirth在英国苏格兰结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1548年5月在英国苏格兰享年37岁。

nameMeanings.name: Campbell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Campell
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: variant of   Campbell   (see also   Camel  ).

variants.name: Beauchamp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Camel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Napoleon, Patrice.

English (of Norman origin) and French: nickname from Norman French came(i)l ‘camel’ (from Latin camelus, classical Greek kamēlos), possibly denoting a clumsy person or someone with long legs. It may also be a topographic or habitational name referring to a house with a sign depicting a camel (although surnames derived from house and inn signs are rare in English); compare 5 below.
English: possibly a habitational name from Queen Camel and West Camel in Somerset, early recorded as Camel(le), possibly a Celtic name from canto- ‘border, district’ and mēl ‘bare hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Campbell of Loudoun was born about 1512, in Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland, her father, Hugh Campbell 15th of Loudoun - Sheriff of Ayr, was 43 and her mother, Isobel Wallace of Craigie, was 27. She married Sir James "Robert" Chalmers of Gadgirth in Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died in May 1548, in Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 37.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Campbell of Loudoun est née environ 1512, à Loudoun, Ayrshire, Écosse, son père, Hugh Campbell 15th of Loudoun - Sheriff of Ayr, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Isobel Wallace of Craigie, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé Sir James "Robert" Chalmers of Gadgirth à Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en mai 1548, à Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 37 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Campbell of Loudoun um 1512 in Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hugh Campbell 15th of Loudoun - Sheriff of Ayr 43 und ihre Mutter Isobel Wallace of Craigie 27. Sie hat in Schottland, United Kingdom Sir James "Robert" Chalmers of Gadgirth geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb im Mai 1548 im Alter von 37 Jahren in Schottland, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Campbell of Loudoun è nata il circa 1512 nel Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland, sua padre, Hugh Campbell 15th of Loudoun - Sheriff of Ayr, aveva 43 anni e sua madre, Isobel Wallace of Craigie, aveva 27 anni. Lei sposato Sir James "Robert" Chalmers of Gadgirth nel Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel maggio 1548, nel Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 37 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Campbell of Loudounが約1512年にスコットランドエイルシュアイアロウドウンで生まれた時，父Hugh Campbell 15th of Loudoun - Sheriff of Ayrは43歳，母Isobel Wallace of Craigieは27歳であった。イギリススコットランドでSir James "Robert" Chalmers of Gadgirthと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1548年5月にイギリススコットランドで37歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Campbell of Loudoun(이)가 대략 1512년 스컷랜드 에이르실르 루다운에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hugh Campbell 15th of Loudoun - Sheriff of Ayr(은)는 43세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isobel Wallace of Craigie(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 영국 스코틀랜드에서 Sir James "Robert" Chalmers of Gadgirth(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1548년 5월 영국 스코틀랜드에서 37세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Campbell of Loudoun nasceu aproximadamente 1512, em Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland, seu pai, Hugh Campbell 15th of Loudoun - Sheriff of Ayr, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Isobel Wallace of Craigie, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir James "Robert" Chalmers of Gadgirth em Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em maio de 1548, em Escócia, United Kingdom, com 37 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Campbell of Loudoun родилась около 1512 г. в местности лоудоун, аиршир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Hugh Campbell 15th of Loudoun - Sheriff of Ayr было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Isobel Wallace of Craigie, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir James "Robert" Chalmers of Gadgirth в местности Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла май 1548 г. в местности Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 37 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Campbell of Loudoun nació aproximadamente 1512, en Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland, su papá, Hugh Campbell 15th of Loudoun - Sheriff of Ayr, tenía 43 años y su mamá, Isobel Wallace of Craigie, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Sir James "Robert" Chalmers of Gadgirth en Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en mayo de 1548, en Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 37 años.
